import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Create a request with a User-Agent header to mimic a browser and avoid 403 blocks
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'}
        )

        # Create SSL context using certifi for up-to-date certificates
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with a timeout
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Detect charset from headers, defaulting to utf-8 if not found
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
            # Use 'replace' to handle undecodable characters gracefully
            content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # We look for all meta tags to handle cases where attributes are in different orders
        description = "No description available"
        meta_tags = re.findall(r'<meta[^>]+>', content, re.IGNORECASE)
        
        for tag in meta_tags:
            # Check if the meta tag has name="description" (case insensitive)
            # This regex handles whitespace around the equals sign
            if re.search(r'name\s*=\s*["\']description["\']', tag, re.IGNORECASE):
                # If it is a description tag, extract the content attribute
                content_match = re.search(r'content\s*=\s*["\'](.*?)["\']', tag, re.IGNORECASE)
                if content_match:
                    description = content_match.group(1).strip()
                    break

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ValueError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

from typing import Dict, Any

import ssl

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate the URL scheme to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL protocol'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

"""
Social Media Scraper Module
Provides functionality to fetch and extract metadata from external webpages.
"""
import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser
from typing import Dict, Optional


class _MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    Internal helper class to parse HTML and extract title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title: str = ""
        self.description: str = ""
        self._in_title_tag: bool = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, Optional[str]]]) -> None:
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            # Convert list of attributes to a dictionary for easier lookup
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check for standard meta description tag
            if attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower() == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str) -> None:
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:

    Args:

              if the tags are not found.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the URL is invalid.
        urllib.error.URLError: If the network request fails.
        RuntimeError: For other unexpected processing errors.

            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
            
            # Read and decode the HTML content
            html_bytes = response.read()
            html_content = html_bytes.decode(charset, errors='replace')

            # Parse the HTML to extract metadata
            parser = _MetadataParser()
            parser.feed(html_content)

            return {
                "title": parser.title,
                "description": parser.description
            }

    except urllib.error.URLError as e:

    except ValueError as e:

        raise ValueError(f"Invalid URL provided: {page_url}") from e
    except Exception as e:
        # Catch-all for other unexpected issues
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to scrape metadata from {page_url}: {e}") from e

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Ensure the URL has a valid scheme (http or https)
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme:
            page_url = 'https://' + page_url

        # Create a Request object with a User-Agent header to mimic a browser
        # This helps avoid 403 Forbidden errors from servers that block scripts
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'}
        )

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        # Added timeout to prevent indefinite hanging
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Attempt to detect charset from headers, default to utf-8 if not found
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if not charset:
                charset = 'utf-8'
            
            content = response.read().decode(charset)

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # Regex now handles both attribute orders:
        # 1. <meta name="description" content="...">
        # 2. <meta content="..." name="description">
        desc_pattern = (
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
            r'|<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']'
        )
        desc_match = re.search(desc_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        
        description = "No description available"
        if desc_match:
            # Depending on which part of the regex matched, the content is in group 1 or 2
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip()

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Create an SSL context using certifi for better certificate validation
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

        # Create a Request object with a User-Agent header to mimic a browser.
        # This helps avoid 403 Forbidden errors from servers that block default Python urllib requests.
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})

        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context) as response:
            # Attempt to get the charset from the response headers, defaulting to 'utf-8'
            # to prevent UnicodeDecodeError on pages with different encodings.
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            content = response.read().decode(charset)

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description.
        # This regex handles two cases:
        # 1. name="description" comes before content="..."
        # 2. content="..." comes before name="description"
        desc_pattern = re.compile(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']|'
            r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']',
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        desc_match = desc_pattern.search(content)
        
        description = ""
        if desc_match:
            # group(1) matches the content if name comes first, group(2) if content comes first
            description = desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)
            description = description.strip()
        
        if not description:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        # Added timeout to prevent indefinite hanging on unresponsive servers
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Attempt to detect charset from headers to handle various encodings
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            charset = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                charset = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip()

            raw_content = response.read()
            try:
                content = raw_content.decode(charset)
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                # Fallback to utf-8 with error replacement if the detected charset fails
                content = raw_content.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # Updated regex to handle both attribute orders: name="desc" content="..." and content="..." name="desc"
        desc_pattern = (r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
                        r'|<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']')
        desc_match = re.search(desc_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        
        description = "No description available"
        if desc_match:
            # group(1) matches the first pattern (name then content)
            # group(2) matches the second pattern (content then name)
            description = desc_match.group(1) if desc_match.group(1) else desc_match.group(2)
            description = description.strip()

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, socket.timeout):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        # Added socket.timeout to handle network timeouts gracefully
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Validate URL scheme to prevent file:// or other protocols
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
            
        if not parsed_url.hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid hostname")

        # Resolve hostname to IP addresses to prevent SSRF
        # We check all resolved IPs to ensure none are private/local
        addr_info = socket.getaddrinfo(parsed_url.hostname, None)
        for family, _, _, _, sockaddr in addr_info:
            ip_str = sockaddr[0]
            try:
                ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
                # Block private, loopback, and link-local addresses
                if ip_obj.is_private or ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_link_local:
                    raise ValueError("Access to private or local addresses is denied")
            except ValueError:
                # If IP parsing fails (unlikely for getaddrinfo results), continue or raise
                continue

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        # Added a timeout to prevent hanging on slow connections
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, socket.gaierror):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched, parsed, or is invalid
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL has a scheme
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme:
            page_url = 'https://' + page_url
        
        # Create a request with headers
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
            }
        )
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with timeout
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content_bytes = response.read()
            
            # Try to determine encoding from Content-Type header
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            charset = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                charset = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip()
            
            try:
                content = content_bytes.decode(charset)
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                # Fallback to utf-8 with error replacement
                content = content_bytes.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description - improved regex to handle more variations
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*(?:name|property)=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']|'
            r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*(?:name|property)=["\']description["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        
        if desc_match:
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2) or "").strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ValueError, OSError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

def _is_valid_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validates the URL to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery) and LFI (Local File Inclusion).
    Only allows http/https schemes and blocks requests to private/internal IP addresses.
    """
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
        
        # Restrict schemes to http and https only
        if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return False
        
        # Ensure hostname is present
        if not parsed.hostname:
            return False
        
        # Explicitly block localhost
        if parsed.hostname.lower() == 'localhost':
            return False
        
        # Check if the hostname is an IP address
        try:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(parsed.hostname)
            # Block private, loopback, link-local, and reserved IPs
            if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local or ip.is_reserved:
                return False
        except ValueError:
            # Hostname is a domain name, not an IP. 
            # Note: This does not prevent DNS rebinding attacks, but mitigates direct IP attacks.
            pass
            
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate the URL before making any requests
    if not _is_valid_url(page_url):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid or restricted URL'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Security Fix: Validate URL to prevent SSRF
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # 1. Restrict schemes to http and https only
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")

        # 2. Validate hostname presence
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid hostname")

        # 3. Resolve hostname to IP address
        try:
            ip_address = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
        except socket.gaierror:
            raise ValueError("DNS resolution failed")

        # 4. Check if IP is private, loopback, link-local, or reserved
        ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_address)
        if ip_obj.is_private or ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_link_local or ip_obj.is_reserved:
            raise ValueError("Access to internal addresses is forbidden")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error

from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and meta description from a webpage.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            # Convert list of tuples to dictionary for easier access
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check for meta description tag (case-insensitive check for name)
            name_attr = attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower()
            if name_attr == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title_tag:
            self.title += data

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme or parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Invalid URL scheme. Use http or https.'
            }
        
        # Create SSL context with certifi certificates
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Create request with User-Agent header
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Try to get encoding from headers, default to utf-8
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                encoding = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip()
            
            content = response.read().decode(encoding, errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description - handle both name and content orderings
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']|<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']',
            content,
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        
        if desc_match:
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ValueError, ssl.SSLError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

from html.parser import HTMLParser


class _MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            # Convert list of tuples to dictionary for easier access
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check for meta description tag (case-insensitive for name)
            name = attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower()
            if name == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title_tag:
            self.title += data

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches HTML content from the given URL and extracts the title and description.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Example: {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the URL cannot be fetched or is invalid.
    """
    # Create an SSL context using certifi to ensure HTTPS requests work reliably
    # across different environments.
    ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=ssl_context) as response:
            # Read the response content. We assume UTF-8, but ignore errors
            # to prevent crashes on malformed encoding.
            html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Failed to fetch URL {page_url}: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f"An unexpected error occurred while fetching {page_url}: {e}")

    parser = _MetadataParser()
    parser.feed(html_content)

    return {
        "title": parser.title.strip(),
        "description": parser.description.strip()
    }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme to prevent SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return {
                'title': 'Invalid URL scheme',
                'description': 'Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are allowed'
            }

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            raw_data = response.read()
            
            # Attempt to decode using the charset specified in headers, defaulting to utf-8
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            try:
                content = raw_data.decode(charset if charset else 'utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                # Fallback to latin-1 (which never fails) if charset detection or decoding fails
                content = raw_data.decode('latin-1')

        # Extract title (allowing for attributes in the tag)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        # This regex handles both attribute orders: name="desc" content="..." and content="..." name="desc"
        desc_pattern = (
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
            r'|<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']'
        )
        desc_match = re.search(desc_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        
        if desc_match:
            # group(1) matches if name comes first, group(2) if content comes first
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def _is_safe_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validates the URL to prevent SSRF and local file access.
    Ensures the scheme is http/https and the host is not a private/internal IP.
    """
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
        
        # Only allow http and https schemes
        if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return False
        
        # Ensure a hostname exists
        if not parsed.hostname:
            return False
            
        # Prevent localhost access
        if parsed.hostname.lower() == 'localhost':
            return False
            
        # Prevent access to private, loopback, and link-local IPs
        try:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(parsed.hostname)
            if ip.is_loopback or ip.is_private or ip.is_link_local:
                return False
        except ValueError:
            # Hostname is not an IP address (e.g., example.com), 
            # so we proceed assuming DNS resolution points to a public IP.
            # Note: This does not protect against DNS rebinding attacks.
            pass
            
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL security before processing
    if not _is_safe_url(page_url):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid or restricted URL'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and description meta tag content.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self._in_title = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            # Convert list of tuples to dict for easier lookup
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            if attrs_dict.get("name") == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title:
            self.title += data

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the HTML content of the given URL and extracts title and description.

    Args:
        page_url (str): The URL of the webpage to scrape.

    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing the 'title' and 'description'.
    """
    # Create a secure SSL context using certifi's CA bundle.
    # This ensures that the SSL certificate of the target URL is verified
    # against a trusted certificate authority, preventing MitM attacks.
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    # Fetch the page content.
    # A timeout is set to prevent the application from hanging indefinitely.
    with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
        # Read and decode the response. errors='replace' handles encoding issues gracefully.
        html_content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

    # Parse the HTML to extract metadata.
    parser = MetadataParser()
    parser.feed(html_content)

    return {
        "title": parser.title.strip(),
        "description": parser.description.strip()
    }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self._in_title = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            # Convert list of attributes to a dictionary for easier lookup
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check for standard description meta tag
            if attrs_dict.get("name") == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title = False

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title:
            self.title += data


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Prepare request with a User-Agent to avoid being blocked by some servers
        headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Attempt to detect charset from headers, defaulting to utf-8
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            content = response.read().decode(charset)

        # Use HTMLParser to extract metadata (more robust than regex)
        parser = MetadataParser()
        parser.feed(content)
        parser.close()

        title = parser.title.strip() if parser.title else "No title found"
        description = parser.description.strip() if parser.description else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Security Fix: Validate URL scheme to prevent SSRF and local file access (e.g., file://)
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL scheme'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

from html.parser import HTMLParser


class _MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """
    A simple HTML parser to extract the title and meta description.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            # Convert list of attributes to a dictionary for easier lookup
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check for the 'description' meta tag (case-insensitive for name)
            if attrs_dict.get("name", "").lower() == "description":
                self.description = attrs_dict.get("content", "")

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title_tag:
            self.title += data


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches HTML content from the given URL and extracts the title and description.

    Args:
        page_url (str): A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description'.
              Returns empty strings for keys if extraction fails or URL is invalid.
    """
    try:
        # Create an SSL context using certifi to ensure proper certificate verification
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

        # Open the URL and read the content
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Determine character encoding from headers, defaulting to utf-8
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
            html_content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Parse the HTML content
        parser = _MetadataParser()
        parser.feed(html_content)

        return {
            'title': parser.title.strip(),
            'description': parser.description.strip()
        }

    except Exception:
        # Return empty structure on any error (network, parsing, etc.)
        return {
            'title': '',
            'description': ''
        }
